ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Your feeling of being healthy?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: The things you eat and drink?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Your work, including jobs, house work, chores, or errands?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Playing sports, gardening, or other physical recreation or hobbies?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Quiet recreation or hobbies, such as reading, TV, music, knitting, etc.?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Your financial situation?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Your relationship with your spouse or domestic partner?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Your sex life?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Your relationship and social activities with your family?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Social activities with your friends, neighbors, or groups?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Your religious or spiritual activities?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Your involvement in community or civic activities?
How much does your illness(es) and/or its treatment interfere with: Your self-improvement or self-expression activities?
IIRS Physical Well-Being and Diet Score
IIRS Work and Finances Score
IIRS Marital, Sexual, and Family Relations Score
Notes: Sum of iirs_7, iirs_8 and iirs_9

ElementDescription: IIRS Recreation and Social Relations Score
Notes: Sum of iirs_4, iirs_5 and iirs_10

ElementDescription: IIRS Other Aspects of Life Score
Notes: Sum of iirs_11, iirs_12 and iirs_13

ElementDescription: IIRS Total Score
Notes: Sum of iirs_1, iirs_2, iirs_3, iirs_4, iirs_5, iirs_6, iirs_7, iirs_8, iirs_9, iirs_10, iirs_11, iirs_12, and iirs_13
